name: Elena Marlowe
title: Bat foraging ecology in urban green spaces
abstract: Urban green spaces have become critical refugia for insectivorous bats, yet the factors governing species-specific habitat use remain poorly understood. We surveyed 12 city parks across three European cities using acoustic detectors and found that tree canopy cover and proximity to water bodies were the strongest predictors of bat activity, explaining 67% of variance in nightly call rates.

name: Elena Marlowe
title: Acoustic monitoring of Pipistrellus species
abstract: Full-spectrum acoustic monitoring was deployed across a rural–urban gradient to characterise niche partitioning among sympatric Pipistrellus species. Call libraries were compared using dynamic time warping, revealing significant spectral overlap between P. pygmaeus and P. pipistrellus in intermediate-intensity urban zones, with implications for automated species identification pipelines.

name: Elena Marlowe
title: Roost selection by Myotis daubentonii along lowland waterways
abstract: We tracked 34 radio-tagged Daubenton's bats over two breeding seasons to characterise roost switching behaviour along canalised versus naturalised river reaches. Roost persistence was significantly lower in canalised sections, and bats commuting from these roosts travelled 1.8x further to reach preferred foraging habitat.

name: Elena Marlowe
title: Light pollution effects on bat community composition in suburban corridors
abstract: Using paired-site acoustic surveys across 40 suburban greenway corridors, we quantified shifts in bat community composition under varying artificial light at night (ALAN) intensities. Light-tolerant genera such as Pipistrellus increased in relative activity under high ALAN, while light-averse Myotis and Plecotus species showed activity reductions of up to 54%.

name: Raj Kapadia
title: CRISPR-Cas9 off-target effects in primary haematopoietic cells
abstract: Off-target editing remains a key safety concern for therapeutic genome editing. Using CIRCLE-seq and targeted amplicon sequencing in CD34+ progenitor cells, we identified 23 reproducible off-target sites for a clinically relevant sgRNA targeting BCL11A, of which 4 fell within annotated regulatory elements, underscoring the need for cell-type-specific off-target profiling.

name: Raj Kapadia
title: Base editing efficiency across chromatin accessibility states
abstract: We systematically evaluated adenine base editor (ABE8e) efficiency at 1,200 genomic loci stratified by ATAC-seq accessibility in K562 and HEK293T cells. Editing efficiency correlated positively with chromatin accessibility (r = 0.61, p < 0.001) and was further modulated by local GC content and distance from the PAM-distal end of the protospacer.

name: Raj Kapadia
title: Prime editing correction of pathogenic SERPINA1 variants in iPSC-derived hepatocytes
abstract: We applied PE3b prime editing to correct the Z allele of SERPINA1 in patient-derived induced pluripotent stem cells, achieving 41% precise correction without detectable indels at the target locus. Corrected hepatocyte-like cells showed restored alpha-1 antitrypsin secretion and reduced ER stress marker expression.

name: Raj Kapadia
title: Long-read sequencing reveals structural variant signatures of Cas9 large deletions
abstract: Short-read sequencing underestimates the frequency of large on-target deletions following Cas9 editing. Using long-read nanopore sequencing across 15 edited loci, we detected megabase-scale deletions and complex rearrangements in 6% of clones, at rates 3-4x higher than short-read amplicon sequencing estimates.

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
title: Machine learning prediction of preterm birth from microbiome data
abstract: Preterm birth affects 11% of pregnancies globally and its aetiology remains multifactorial. We trained gradient-boosted classifiers on 16S rRNA amplicon profiles from 780 pregnant individuals sampled at 16 weeks gestation, achieving AUROC 0.81 for spontaneous preterm birth before 34 weeks, with Lactobacillus crispatus abundance and community state type as the top predictive features.

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
title: Bat foraging ecology in urban green spaces
abstract: Urban green spaces have become critical refugia for insectivorous bats, yet the factors governing species-specific habitat use remain poorly understood. We surveyed 12 city parks across three European cities using acoustic detectors and found that tree canopy cover and proximity to water bodies were the strongest predictors of bat activity, explaining 67% of variance in nightly call rates.

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
title: Vaginal microbiome dynamics across the second and third trimester
abstract: Longitudinal 16S profiling of 210 pregnancies from 20 to 36 weeks gestation revealed that transitions away from Lactobacillus-dominant community state types were significantly associated with subsequent spontaneous preterm birth, with the steepest divergence occurring between 24 and 28 weeks.

name: Priya Chandrasekaran
title: Antibiotic exposure and gut microbiome maturation in preterm infants
abstract: We characterised gut microbiome development in 95 preterm infants exposed to varying durations of empirical antibiotics in the neonatal intensive care unit. Extended antibiotic courses (>5 days) delayed the establishment of a Bifidobacterium-dominant community by a median of 3 weeks relative to infants receiving shorter courses.
